title: Fundamentos da economia, mercado financeiro e intenção de voto: as eleições presidenciais brasileiras de 1994, 1998 e 2002
value: O corpo de "Fundamentos da economia, mercado financeiro e intenção de voto: as eleições presidenciais brasileiras de 1994, 1998 e 2002" analisa dados quantitativos, administrativos ou de survey com procedimento empírico próprio ou secundário claramente descrito. A classificação é conservadora: detalhes causais, variáveis e identificação só foram preenchidos quando o texto os declarou de forma explícita.
previous_value: The XML contains only metadata (title, abstracts, references) with no article body text, so classification relies on the English and French abstracts. The paper is an empirical quantitative study of economic voting in Brazilian presidential elections (1994, 1998, 2002), testing hypotheses about the relationship between macroeconomic indicators and aggregate voting intentions using time-series data. The abstract describes testing simultaneity between economic indicators and vote intention, consistent with a VAR or similar time-series approach, and concludes that retrospective voting based on economic fundamentals (not financial markets) predominates in Brazil.

value: O corpo de "O conceito de federalismo e a idéia de interesse no Brasil do século XIX" se apresenta como ensaio teórico, conceitual ou de história das ideias, sem análise própria de dados observacionais ou experimentais. previous_value: This is a conceptual history (Begriffsgeschichte) article using Koselleck's methodology to trace the evolution of the concept of federalism in 19th century Brazil. It analyzes newspapers and parliamentary debates as primary sources but employs no quantitative methods. The work is interpretive and historical-theoretical, focused on political concepts and their semantic transformations rather than empirical hypothesis testing.

previous_value: Qualitative sociological study using ethnographic fieldwork in four high schools in Seine-Saint-Denis to analyze how Sciences Po outreach programs affect students' perceptions of meritocracy and inequality. The article draws on the French sociology of education tradition (Dubet, Van Zanten, Oberti) and employs qualitative methods to examine reception mechanisms of an unprecedented educational offer in stigmatized urban environments.

title: Continuidade, Ruptura ou Reciclagem? Uma Análise do Programa Político do Banco Mundial após o Consenso de Washington
value: O corpo de "Continuidade, Ruptura ou Reciclagem? Uma Análise do Programa Político do Banco Mundial após o Consenso de Washington" conduz análise empírica qualitativa ou histórico-documental, não uma análise quantitativa de dados observacionais ou experimentais. Os campos estatísticos e de desenho causal foram preenchidos apenas quando o método estava claramente declarado no texto.
previous_value: This is a theoretical-analytical essay that traces the evolution of the World Bank's political program from the Washington Consensus era (1989) through 2014. It relies on documentary analysis of the Bank's own publications and secondary literature, with no quantitative data, statistical analysis, or empirical testing. The article argues the Bank's agenda was 'recycled' rather than fundamentally changed, incorporating neo-institutionalist ideas to expand its liberal reform prescriptions across economy, state, civil society, and individual subjectivity in client countries. There are zero tables, figures, or equations (confirmed by counts in XML metadata). The 86 references are predominantly books and critical political economy journals. The method is interpretive-historical and essayistic throughout.

title: Organizar a Desordem: Raízes do Brasil em 1936
value: O corpo de "Organizar a Desordem: Raízes do Brasil em 1936" se apresenta como ensaio teórico, conceitual ou de história das ideias, sem análise própria de dados observacionais ou experimentais. Por isso, os campos de desenho causal, variáveis, amostra e resultados estatísticos foram deixados nulos.
previous_value: This is a work of Brazilian political thought/intellectual history. The author reconstructs the arguments of Sergio Buarque de Holanda's Raízes do Brasil (1936), tracing his intellectual dialogue with Oliveira Vianna and Gilberto Freyre around concepts of cordiality, political doctrines vs. national reality, and the idea of 'organizing disorder.' There is no quantitative data, no empirical hypothesis testing, and no causal research design. The method is interpretive-hermeneutic essay based on close reading of primary texts.

title: Entre a Desinstitucionalização e a Resiliência: Participação Institucional no Governo Bolsonaro
value: O corpo de "Entre a Desinstitucionalização e a Resiliência: Participação Institucional no Governo Bolsonaro" conduz análise empírica qualitativa ou histórico-documental, não uma análise quantitativa de dados observacionais ou experimentais. Os campos estatísticos e de desenho causal foram preenchidos apenas quando o método estava claramente declarado no texto.
previous_value: Mixed-methods study of 103 Brazilian federal participatory councils under Bolsonaro (2019-2022). Uses original legal/administrative dataset to classify councils by normative and activity status across policy areas. Develops a typology of institutional resilience based on design strength and policy community insertion. Primarily descriptive/typological with qualitative comparative logic rather than formal quantitative causal inference. Implicit causal claims about government preferences and institutional design driving differential deinstitutionalization, but no regression models, statistical tests, or formal causal design.

title: Memória e historicidade em dois "comandos" prisionais
journal_title: Lua Nova: Revista de Cultura e Política
value: O corpo de "Memória e historicidade em dois "comandos" prisionais" conduz análise empírica qualitativa ou histórico-documental, não uma análise quantitativa de dados observacionais ou experimentais. Os campos estatísticos e de desenho causal foram preenchidos apenas quando o método estava claramente declarado no texto.
previous_value: This is a qualitative anthropological study that constructs a fictional dialogue between prisoners affiliated with two São Paulo prison commands (PCC and CRBC) to map different historicities and memories about prison 'wars'. It draws on ethnographic fieldwork and uses no quantitative methods, statistical analysis, or causal inference. The authors employ ethnographic/participant observation methodology grounded in anthropological theory (Lévi-Strauss, Viveiros de Castro, Deleuze & Guattari) to explore competing narratives and viewpoints among prisoners.

title: O local e o global na estrutura da política ambiental internacional: a construção social do acidente químico ampliado de Bhopal e da Convenção 174 da OIT
value: O corpo de "O local e o global na estrutura da política ambiental internacional: a construção social do acidente químico ampliado de Bhopal e da Convenção 174 da OIT" conduz análise empírica qualitativa ou histórico-documental, não uma análise quantitativa de dados observacionais ou experimentais. Os campos estatísticos e de desenho causal foram preenchidos apenas quando o método estava claramente declarado no texto.
previous_value: This is a qualitative constructivist International Relations paper that analyzes the social construction of the Bhopal chemical disaster and its normative impact leading to ILO Convention 174. It employs no quantitative methods, datasets, or statistical analysis. The paper uses theoretical interpretation (constructivism, co-constitution of agents and structures) and historical/documentary analysis of international environmental norms, conferences (Stockholm 1972, Rio 1992), and the role of ILO. It is a single-case interpretive study in the IR subfield of International Environmental Politics.

title: Relevant Factors for the Voting Decision in the 2002 Presidential Election: An Analysis of the ESEB (Brazilian Electoral Study) Data
value: O corpo de "Relevant Factors for the Voting Decision in the 2002 Presidential Election: An Analysis of the ESEB (Brazilian Electoral Study) Data" analisa dados quantitativos, administrativos ou de survey com procedimento empírico próprio ou secundário claramente descrito. A classificação é conservadora: detalhes causais, variáveis e identificação só foram preenchidos quando o texto os declarou de forma explícita.
previous_value: This is a quantitative electoral behavior study of the 2002 Brazilian presidential election using post-electoral survey data (ESEB) and logistic regression models. The paper tests hypotheses about factors driving vote choice (religion, partisanship, ideology, government evaluation, candidate attributes) for four candidates. It uses a kitchen-sink logistic regression approach with multiple blocks of predictors rather than a causal identification strategy. The language uses 'explain' in quotes, suggesting awareness that the associations are not strictly causal, but the framing of 'relevant factors for the voting decision' and 'determinants' implies causal reasoning without formal identification.

title: Transnational Social Movements and the Globalization Agenda: A Methodological Approach Based on the Analysis of the World Social Forum
value: O corpo de "Transnational Social Movements and the Globalization Agenda: A Methodological Approach Based on the Analysis of the World Social Forum" se apresenta como ensaio teórico, conceitual ou de história das ideias, sem análise própria de dados observacionais ou experimentais. Por isso, os campos de desenho causal, variáveis, amostra e resultados estatísticos foram deixados nulos.
previous_value: This is a theoretical paper that proposes a conceptual and methodological framework for analyzing transnational social movements, using the World Social Forum as an illustrative case. It contains no quantitative data, no statistical analysis, no tables or figures, and relies entirely on theoretical argumentation drawing from political theory (Habermas, Rawls, Arendt, Touraine) and social movement literature. title: The United Nations System and the Process of Political Articulation of Local Authorities as a Global Actor

previous_value: This article analyzes the interaction between local authorities (organized as UCLG) and the United Nations system, examining how subnational governments achieved political objectives despite limited power resources. It is a qualitative, descriptive-analytical piece grounded in International Relations theory (transnational actors, paradiplomacy, norm creation literature). There is no quantitative data, no statistical analysis, and no formal research design. The paper uses a theoretical framework of 'mixed actors' (sovereignty-bound and sovereignty-free) to explain the political articulation of local authorities as global actors. It is essayistic in method, relying on documentary analysis and theoretical argumentation rather than systematic empirical methods.

title: Social Scientists and Public Administration in the Lula da Silva Government
value: O corpo de "Social Scientists and Public Administration in the Lula da Silva Government" analisa dados quantitativos, administrativos ou de survey com procedimento empírico próprio ou secundário claramente descrito. previous_value: This article examines the presence of social scientists in high-level federal government positions during the Lula da Silva administration in Brazil. While the authors mention 'empirical research' in the abstract, the paper is fundamentally a descriptive, qualitative study of elite composition and academic backgrounds in public administration. It does not employ quantitative statistical methods, regression models, or causal inference designs. The hypothesis offered — that social scientists' presence is explained more by links to public service than by disciplinary knowledge — is framed discursively rather than tested with formal quantitative methods. The paper fits Public Policy/Administration given its focus on bureaucratic elites and government staffing.

previous_value: This is a methodological paper that introduces the p-value statistic and demonstrates, through simulations, replications, and observational data, when statistical significance is not meaningful. It argues that graphical analysis should precede p-value interpretation, p-values are pointless for non-random samples and population data, and are highly sensitive to sample size. It does not test a substantive political science hypothesis and has no causal research design. It references Cohen's power analysis book, hence the power analysis flag. The evidence is quantitative (simulations and data demonstrations) with explicit methodology.

previous_value: This is a descriptive empirical paper that uses survey microdata (POF 2008-2009) to characterize the socio-economic profile of Brazil's so-called 'new middle class' through consumption and living standard indicators. It does not estimate causal effects or seek determinants; rather, it challenges the classification of a particular income stratum as 'middle class' by showing their consumption patterns resemble vulnerable/poor strata more than traditional middle class. No regression models, causal claims, or identification strategies are employed -- the analysis is purely descriptive and classificatory.
